body.title: Gate PROCEDURAL habit writes on skill telemetry outcome rate (Schmidhuber)
body.result: closed — pe_chain coding mechanism decommissioned; ticket no longer relevant to reasoning-first direction
body.description:
Skill telemetry schema, contracts, logger, and rollup all shipped (T-skill-telemetry-* closed). The Schmidhuber gate: before writing a PROCEDURAL memory (habit), check whether the habit's code_ref function has skill telemetry outcome data. If it does and the pass rate < 0.40 (habit actively hurts more than it helps), post to cc_inbox and do not write. If no telemetry exists: write normally (can't block what we can't measure). This prevents accumulation of 'sounds good but unverified' habits.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/memory_store.py (write gate for PROCEDURAL type), wild_igor/igor/tools/skill_telemetry.py (read outcome rate by code_ref)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/memory, theigors/rules/database
**Scope boundary:** In scope: PROCEDURAL write gate using existing telemetry data. Out of scope: retroactively scoring existing habits, changing what code_ref means.
**Test plan:** Mock telemetry returning 0.3 pass rate for a code_ref; verify habit write is blocked and cc_inbox entry posted. Mock telemetry returning 0.8; verify write proceeds. Mock no telemetry; verify write proceeds.
